decisions.title: Project Root Level Organization
decisions.summary: The project is organized at the root level into distinct directories for client, server, item-images service, documentation, scripts, docker configuration, and a development launcher script.

decisions.title: Client Directory Structure
decisions.summary: The client directory is structured into components, systems, stores, views, utils, assets, and infrastructure as code directories to separate concerns in the Vue.js frontend application.

decisions.title: Server Directory Structure
decisions.summary: The server directory contains handlers, AI integration, state persistence, utilities, infrastructure as code, and tests to organize the Bun WebSocket server codebase.

decisions.title: Item Images Service Structure
decisions.summary: The item-images service is organized into handlers, core library, state management, and infrastructure as code directories to support AI image generation.

decisions.title: Frontend Systems Architecture
decisions.summary: The frontend uses a system-based architecture with independent systems communicating via events, including socket, physics, game object, and item/inventory systems.

decisions.title: Message-Driven Communication Pattern
decisions.summary: WebSocket messages use kebab-case naming, server handlers are organized by message type, and frontend updates are event-driven to maintain reactive state.

decisions.title: Infrastructure as Code per Service
decisions.summary: Each service contains its own iac directory with CloudFormation templates, deployment scripts, and environment-specific configurations to manage infrastructure as code.

decisions.title: Testing Structure
decisions.summary: Server tests are located in __tests__ directories alongside source code using Bun test framework; client tests use Vitest with Vue Test Utils; test configurations are in package.json files.

decisions.title: File Naming Conventions
decisions.summary: File names, component names, and message types use kebab-case; JavaScript/TypeScript variables and functions use camelCase; Vue components and TypeScript interfaces use PascalCase; TypeScript is preferred with JavaScript for legacy word lists.

rules.rule: Use kebab-case for file names, component names, and WebSocket message types.
Use camelCase for JavaScript and TypeScript variables and functions.
Use PascalCase for Vue components and TypeScript interfaces.
Organize server WebSocket message handlers by message type in kebab-case.
Place server tests in __tests__ directories alongside source code using Bun test framework.
Use Vitest with Vue Test Utils for client tests; configure tests in package.json.
Each service must have an iac directory containing CloudFormation YAML templates, deployment scripts, and environment-specific configurations.
